name: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
description: EU regulation classifying AI systems by risk level. Full enforcement: August 2, 2026.

criteria.summary: AI systems posing unacceptable risk. Banned in the EU.
examples: Real-time biometric identification in public spaces (with narrow exceptions)
Exploitation of vulnerabilities of specific groups
Biometric categorization based on sensitive characteristics
Untargeted scraping of facial images for facial recognition databases

criteria.summary: AI systems used in specific high-impact sectors. Subject to extensive compliance requirements.

annex_iii_categories.1_biometrics: Biometric identification, categorization, and emotion recognition
annex_iii_categories.2_critical_infrastructure: Critical infrastructure management (traffic, water, gas, electricity)
annex_iii_categories.3_education: Education and vocational training (admission, evaluation, assessment)
annex_iii_categories.4_employment: Employment, worker management, access to self-employment (recruitment, promotion, termination decisions)
annex_iii_categories.5_essential_services: Access to essential services: (a) public benefits eligibility, (b) credit scoring or creditworthiness evaluation of natural persons, (c) risk assessment and pricing of life and health insurance, (d) emergency response services
annex_iii_categories.8_justice_democracy: Administration of justice and democratic processes

criteria.summary: AI systems with specific transparency obligations. Users must be informed they are interacting with AI.
All other AI systems. Voluntary codes of conduct only.
